Position rack in top third of oven and preheat to 177°C. Spray 13x9x2-inch metal baking pan with nonstick spray. Combine oats and next 5 ingredients in large bowl and whisk to blend. Add cubed 'butter.' Using fingertips, blend until mixture forms small moist clumps.
Firmly press half of crust mixture (about 3 cups) over bottom of prepared pan. Spread filling evenly over crust. Sprinkle remaining crust mixture over filling; press to adhere.

Bake bars until deep brown and firm to touch, about 35 minutes. Cool in pan on rack. Cut crosswise into 6 strips. Make 8 lengthwise cuts, forming 2x1-inch bars. DO AHEAD Can be made 1 day ahead. Cover pan with foil; store at room temperature.

Place powdered sugar in medium bowl. Using narrow spatula, remove as many bars as desired from pan. Roll half of bars in powdered sugar to coat. Arrange coated bars and plain bars alternately on platter.
For longer storage, cover pan and chill. Bars will keep one week, but crust will soften.

oatmeal-banana-muffins.name: Oatmeal-banana Muffins
instructions: Preheat the oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Line a muffin tin with paper liners.
Mash bananas in a bowl. Mix in applesauce, egg, sugar, and vanilla extract.

Mix einkorn flour, oats, blueberries, coconut, cinnamon, baking powder, salt, and baking soda together in another bowl. Add to banana mixture and stir until just combined. Spoon batter into the muffin cups.
Bake in the preheated oven until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean, 20 to 25 minutes.

oatmeal-crusted-brownies.name: Oatmeal-crusted Brownies
instructions: Preheat oven to 177°C (175 degrees C). Grease a 9x13-inch baking dish.

Mix oats, 3/4 cup flour, brown sugar, baking soda, and 1/4 teaspoon salt together in a bowl. Stir 3/4 cup melted butter into oat mixture until evenly coated. Measure 3/4 cup oat mixture and reserve for later. Press remaining oat mixture into the prepared baking dish.
Bake in the preheated oven until lightly browned, about 5 minutes.
Combine white sugar, 1 cup melted butter, and vanilla extract in a bowl. Beat eggs, 1 at a time, into sugar mixture, mixing well after each addition, until thoroughly blended.

Sift 1 1/2 cups flour, cocoa powder, and 1 teaspoon salt together in a bowl; stir into sugar-egg mixture just until batter is blended. Fold chocolate chips into batter; pour over oat crust. Sprinkle reserved oat mixture over batter.
Bake in the preheated oven until a toothpick inserted in the center of the brownies comes out clean, 35 to 40 minutes.

oaxaca-old-fashioned-cocktail.name: Oaxaca Old Fashioned Cocktail

ingredients.prep: Del Maguey San Luis del Rio preferred

instructions: Mix the tequila, mezcal, agave nectar, and bitters in a mixing glass with ice.
Strain over a fresh rock in an Old Fashioned glass.
Flame an orange peel over the top of the drink, and rest the twist in the glass as a garnish.

oaxacan-tamales-recipe-comedera.name: Oaxacan Tamales Recipe

ingredients.item: 500 gr de carne de cerdo o pollo deshebrado

instructions: Beat the butter till it's fluffy.
Once ready, it incorporates the dough of corn little by little, alternating with chicken stock, baking powder and salt. The mass should be soft and light.
Cooking pork or chicken till tender, shedding and booking.
Warm up the oaxaquan mole and add the broken meat and mix well till well integrated.
Cut banana leaves into rectangles about 30 cm by 20 cm.
Put a portion of mass at the centre of each leaf and light up. Add a tablespoon of mole with meat at the centre of the dough.
Fold the sides of the banana leaf to the centre and then turn the ends down, and form a well wrapped package.
Coloca los tamales en una vaporera, con la parte doblada hacia abajo.
Fitted steamed for about 1 hour and 30 minutes, or until the dough is firm and easily clear from the banana leaf.

obatzda-the-bavarian-cream-cheese-spread.name: Obatzda, The Bavarian Cream Cheese Spread
obatzda-the-bavarian-cream-cheese-spread.original_name: Obatzda, la crema de queso bávara para untar

ingredients.item: 200 g de queso camembert o queso brie
2 cucharadas de mantequilla sin sal, a temperatura ambiente

instructions: At a large bowl, place Camembert or Brie and cream cheese. Be sure they're at room temperature to facilitate their blend.
with a fork, pits cheeses to get a smooth and homogenous blend.
Add minced onion and butter to room temperature. Keep mixing till all ingredients are well combined.
Incorporates sweet pimenton and ground cumin into the mixture. Remix to distribute flavors evenly.
Test the mixture and bowl with salt and pepper to taste. Adjust the condiments to your personal preferences.
Transfiere el Obatzda a un recipiente adecuado para servir y decora con cebollino picado si lo deseas.
Cover the container with film paper and refrigerate for at least 1 hour so that flavors blend and cool properly.
obatzda-the-bavarian-cream-cheese-spread.category: Recetas de Aperitivos, Tapas y Pinchos

obe-ata-stew-with-chicken-and-spinach.name: Obe Ata Stew With Chicken And Spinach

ingredients.prep: thigh and drumstick; about 3 lb. total
seeds and ribs removed, coarsely chopped
sliced into  0.5" rounds (or 2 medium Yukon Gold potatoes, cut into 1" pieces)

instructions: Heat oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Season chicken with salt and cook (skin side down), undisturbed, until skin is browned and crisp, 8–10 minutes. Turn and cook just until lightly browned, about 3 minutes longer. Using tongs, transfer chicken to a plate (it will not be cooked through).

Cook bell peppers and onion in same pot, stirring often, until onion is golden brown and vegetables are softened, 6–8 minutes. Add tomatoes, chiles, and pepper and cook, stirring often, until tomatoes are broken down and softened, about 6 minutes longer. Transfer to a blender (reserve pot) and pour in broth. Purée until very smooth.

Return purée to pot along with chicken and potatoes. Bring to a simmer over medium-high heat, partially cover pot, and reduce heat to maintain a simmer. Cook until chicken and potatoes are very tender, 45–50 minutes. Season with salt to taste and stir in spinach. Cook, stirring, until spinach is wilted, about 1 minute.
Divide stew among bowls. Spoon rice into soup alongside chicken and chives.

obituary-cocktail.name: Obituary Cocktail
instructions: Combine all the ingredients except the lemon twist in a mixing glass filled with ice and stir until chilled, about 30 seconds. Strain into a chilled coupe. Express a lemon twist over the drink and drop into the glass.

ochazuke-japanese-green-tea-over-rice.name: Ochazuke (japanese Green Tea Over Rice)

ingredients.item: Hulled white or black sesame seeds
. surigoma or a combination of nori and toasted sesame seeds
. grated fresh wasabi or wasabi paste

instructions: Heat a medium dry skillet over medium-low heat. Add the sesame seeds, stirring constantly until the seeds become fragrant and lightly colored (but not browned), about 5 minutes. Black sesame seeds will not turn color but will become fragrant. When a few seeds pop, that is the sign to monitor carefully and start taking them off the heat. Transfer the hot toasted seeds into a mortar and crush the seeds with the pestle until your desired coarseness or smoothness is achieved (or grind them in a food processor). Leave some seeds whole for good texture. Cool completely and store in an airtight container in a cool, dark place or in the refrigerator, where the seeds will keep for up to 1 month.

Preheat the broiler. Line a baking sheet with aluminum foil and brush it with the oil. Season the salmon with salt and pepper. Place the salmon on the baking sheet and broil for about 6 to 10 minutes, to desired doneness. If using smoked salmon, it will cook in half the time. Let cool and flake the meat.

Divide the rice among individual rice bowls and spoon the flaked salmon on top. At the table, pour about 1 cup (240 ml) of the hot tea over each and garnish with the surigoma or nori and sesame seeds, and scallions. Serve topped with the wasabi, if desired.

ochazuke-japanese-steeped-rice.name: Ochazuke (japanese Steeped Rice)

ingredients.item: White soy sauce or 3 tablespoons regular soy sauce

ingredients.prep: such as hojicha, genmaicha, or unro
dark-green parts only, very thinly sliced crosswise
furikake seasoning or toasted sesame seeds, and shredded toasted nori (for serving)

instructions: Scoop ½ cup rice onto a clean surface. Dampen your hands in a small bowl of salted water (this will prevent the rice from sticking to them and season them slightly). Firmly compress rice into a tight ball, then press down on surface to gently flatten to a 2½–3" round. Repeat with remaining rice to form 4 rice cakes.

Heat oil in a large skillet, preferably nonstick, over medium-high. Brush one side of rice cakes with soy sauce. Cook, soy sauce side down, until edges are golden brown and crisp, 5–6 minutes. Brush tops of rice cakes with soy sauce. Turn and cook remaining side until golden brown and crisp, another 5–6 minutes. Remove from heat.

Meanwhile, pour water into a large saucepan to a depth of 2". Bring to a boil, then reduce heat so water is at a gentle simmer. Crack an egg into a small bowl, then gently slide egg into simmering water. Repeat with remaining eggs, waiting until whites of eggs in water are opaque before adding the next (about 30 seconds apart). Poach, shaping with a slotted spoon if needed, until whites are set but yolks are still runny, about 3 minutes. Using slotted spoon, transfer eggs to paper towels as they are done.
Add spinach to saucepan and cook until just wilted, 20–30 seconds. Drain; let cool slightly, then squeeze out excess water with your hands.

Heat 3 cups water in a small saucepan over high until right when water starts to steam (an instant-read thermometer will register 172°). Add tea and let steep 2 minutes. Strain through a fine-mesh sieve into a small bowl; discard solids. Stir in remaining ¼ cup soy sauce.

Divide rice cakes, spinach, and eggs among bowls. Pour tea mixture over, then top with scallions, pickled ginger, furikake, and nori. Finely grate mushroom over with a microplane just before serving, if using.

ocopa-step-by-step-comedera.name: Ocopa, Step By Step

ingredients.item: 150 gr de queso fresco o serrano

instructions: Let's start our recipe, warming up a frying pan with olive oil.
Cut the onion, yellow and yellowish interiors into julians or thin strips. Trocks garlic and huacatay.
When the oil's hot, we're gonna have to drink the garlic, then the onion. Later, the Aids and finally you will add the Huacatay. It's cold for five minutes. Kill and let rest.
In a blender or processor, add evaporate milk. Then you'll have to add some frying vegetables and get out.
Then we'll add minced cheese and cookies that will give you texture. Loan till you have a homogenous and well-processed sauce.
Cut the potatoes in half, and you'll scatter his Ocopa sauce all over his surface.
Now, you'll be ready to eat. Drop it!

octopus-confit-argiro.name: Octopus Confit
instructions: For the octopus knot we will use as small a vessel as possible so that the few liquids are concentrated around the octopus tentacles. With the slow roasting, the octopus will melt into the oil.
Put them in the vessel and add all the other materials. Garlic with its dress, pepper beans, hot pepper (optional) laurel, marinated in slices, tomatoes, oregano or some thyme.
Pour with vinegar and olive oil until the octopus is covered.
Cover with non-stick paper and seal the vessel with aluminum foil or lid to keep all the liquids inside.
Bake at 170oC on the bottom grill for 45' until the octopus softens, but be careful not to melt.
We leave it covered in its zooms to cool.
Yet we prepare the fava.

octopus-cooked-in-red-wine-teleculinaria.name: Octopus Cooked In Red Wine

ingredients.item: Batatas e espinafres cozidos para acompanhar

instructions: Descasque os alhos e corte-os em rodelas. Descasque a cebola e corte-a em tiras.

Place the onion and garlic in a large pot, add the olive oil, the blond, salt, pepper, the wine, octopus and thyme, take it to the fire and let it cook for about 1 hour, with the pot covered and in low heat. Make sure the octopus is cooked, correct the seasonings and remove from the fire.
Transfer the octopus to a plate and cut it into large pieces. Add cooked potatoes and spinach and water everything with the cooking sauce. Serve right away.

octopus-dried-on-coals-argiro.name: Octopus Dried On Coals
instructions: The most beautiful island meze is the octopus glazed in the coals or octopus liocapto as we call it in the islands, which is baked and smells the whole place. It is not necessary for the octopus to be large, but it is necessary to have melted. If you want to eat octopus on the coals, like what we serve on the islands, the octopus should be dried using the following procedure:
Spread the octopus on a rope and let it dry in the hot sun. Essentially sprinkle the octopus with a little vinegar and protect it from insects with a tulpan. Cover it with the tulpan and let it dry.
Depending on the size and how hot the sun will take 3-5 hours. Lumpy octopus in the coals with percussion texture, is characteristic meze on our islands and is a great pleasure.
In winter months, where there is little sunshine, we leave it in the air to dry out of the air.
But how is the sundried octopus baked: You need some balls with medium coal fire and a grill very clean and hot.
The bribe is unnecessary and you don't have to oil either the grill or the octopus. Only the grill must be very clean and the sunny octopus properly dried.
We separate the tentacles by pulling them, and we touch them on the grill.
They want about 5′ to 8′ on each side, depending on the size and thickness of the tentacles.
When bubbles stop coming out of the tentacles on the grill being baked, then the octopus on the coals is ready and properly baked.
After baking, cut the octopus with a knife into diagonal slices and squeeze some lemon. No need for herbs. Enjoy it straight or with a few drops of lemon.
We never put oil on a sunken octopus baked in the coals. It's unnecessary.
Enjoy it while it still has its juices on each piece. We on the island always accompany it with Suma Paros.

octopus-feijoada-in-30-minutes-teleculin.name: Octopus Feijoada In 30 Minutes

ingredients.item: 1 lata pequena  tomate pelado em cubos ((390 g))

instructions: Peel off the onions and garlic, cut them in half-moons and praise them in a pot with the olive oil. Add the pepper, clean and cut into small pieces, and the octopus, cut into equally small pieces. Add the tomato cubes and wrap.
Add the beans drained and let cook, in mild heat, for about 15 minutes (if necessary, add some water). In the end, season with salt and pepper, sprinkle with chopped parsley and serve.

octopus-fillets-with-oven-baked-rice-tel.name: Octopus Fillets With Oven-baked Rice
octopus-fillets-with-oven-baked-rice-tel.original_name: Filetes de polvo com arroz de forno
instructions: Place the octopus in a pot, cover with water, take it to the fire and let it cook for 40 minutes. Remove the octopus from the pot, cool it and then cut it into pieces, and the thicker parts should be cut in half to become a kind of fillets. Season with lemon juice and a pinch of salt and pepper and reserve.
Pass the octopus water through a passer, lay it in a pot and light the light, leaving it boiling.

In a separate pot, praise the onion and garlic chopped into the olive oil, along with the laurel leaf. Add 200 ml of octopus water, cover and let cook for 10 minutes. Then add the rice and finest parts of the octopus as well as another 1.3 l of octopus water. Season with salt, cover and let cook for 5 minutes. Then take the pot in the oven, preheated at 200°C, for 20 minutes.

Pour out the octopus fillets, pass them through flour, then through the beaten eggs, and finally through grated bread. Get them to fry in hot oil until they're golden on both sides. Remove, let it drip on absorbent paper and serve with rice.

octopus-grilled-in-aluminum-foil-argiro.name: Octopus Grilled In Aluminum Foil
instructions: To make the octopus roasted on the foil, clean the octopus first and place it whole in a bowl.
Add the olive oil, balsamic vinegar (or simple vinegar from wine), bay leaf, garlic and oregano and mix all the ingredients together well.
Place two large pieces of aluminum foil on the counter and place on a piece of anti-stick baking paper.
Place the octopus in the middle and pour it with the mixture.
Cross-reference the anti-stick paper and close with the aluminum foil in a package – not tight because it inflates with baking.
Bake the octopus for about 1 hour and 15 minutes to 1 hour and 30 minutes at 180oC and serve.

octopus-honeyed-with-crushed-potatoes-in.name: Octopus Honeyed With Crushed Potatoes In The Oven
octopus-honeyed-with-crushed-potatoes-in.original_name: Χταπόδι μελωμένο με τσακιστές πατάτες στο φούρνο
instructions: Clean and wash the octopus. Drain it in a colander.
In a small pot, add all the ingredients: the cleaned onions, the finely chopped in quarters, garlic cloves, tomatoes, parsley branches, oregano and thyme.
Add the leaves and pepper grains.
Above you put the octopus.
Pour with the olive oil and vinegar.
Cover the pot with a lid that seal well.
Leave the octopus in the pot for about 50’ to soften. We're checking with a knife to see if he's boiled in his fluids and it's tender.
In the middle of the boil the pot will have many liquids from the octopus but will slowly condensate and eventually resemble a thin jelly.
Put the broken potatoes on a baking pan that fits them in a layer.
Add 2-3 tablespoons olive oil, salt and freshly ground pepper.
Add 2 garlic cloves between. Add thyme or oregano.
We touch the cooked octopus on the broken potatoes.
Add the glazed vegetables of the pot between the potatoes.
Pour with the few liquids of the octopus left in the pot.
If we want to add some hot pepper, it fits a lot.
Bake the octopus with the broken potatoes in a well preheated oven at 2003
Optionally, sprinkle with some parsley in the serving.
Serve the octopus with the potatoes warm.

octopus-in-the-oven-with-vegetables-argi.name: Octopus In The Oven With Vegetables

ingredients.item: Two or three branches of parsley
Two or three branches of parsley

instructions: We clean the octopus first. We remove his mouth by entering our marker and pulling. Turn its hood, the inside outside, remove everything in it (holding only the whole, i.e. the egg). Wash very well and cut the octopus into large pieces (leave the tentacles whole if small or cut into bites if large). But remember that at any size you cut it, after cooking it will be reduced by 1/3.
Clean the potatoes, cut them into slices from 0.5 cm to 1 cm thick.
Clean the onions and cut them into rose rings and coat them on the potatoes.
Cut the tomatoes into slices and coat them.
Spread the octopus tentacles up. Add the herbs, laurel, thyme, parsley and garlic.
Salt pepper (with caution because the octopus if it is fresh is usually very salty).
Pour with the olive oil.
Cover with aluminum foil and close the baking pan around so that it keeps all the flavor.
Bake in a well preheated oven for 1 hour and 30 minutes at 170 degrees C. We do not add liquids because the octopus and vegetables will make a lot.

octopus-in-the-shell-with-spinach-and-le.name: Octopus In The Shell With Spinach And Lentil Pasta
octopus-in-the-shell-with-spinach-and-le.original_name: Χταπόδι στη γάστρα με ζυμαρικά σπανάκι και φακές

ingredients.item: 1 large carrot cut into small frames
1 large carrot cut into small frames

instructions: First boil the lentils in plenty of water for about 20 minutes to be half boiled. We drain them well.
Add the half-boiled lentils to the hull to a layer.
We make a layer of spinach cut.
Heat half the olive oil and let the onions soften slightly for 10′.
Add the garlic broken, peppers, carrot and mix.
Add the octopus, turn the heat on and allow it to lower its liquids for a few minutes until it changes color.
Add the balsamic.
Mix well to completely evaporate the vinegar.
Add the ready tomato sauce and let it boil for 10 minutes.
Make an octopus sauce layer on the other ingredients in the hull and mix well.
Cover the hull and bake for 30′.
Add the pasta, mix well and cover and continue baking for 20′ with the pan covered.
When we put the pasta in the pot, there should be enough zum of all the ingredients to cover the pasta. If necessary, add some hot water to boil properly.
Add salt and pepper always at the end because the octopus is enough and as long as it makes its taste in the food.
Sprinkle with very finely chopped parsley and serve.

octopus-lasagna-teleculinaria.name: Octopus Lasagna
instructions: Cook the octopus in water, with the whole peeled onion and blond leaf, for about 40 minutes or until tender. Remove it, reserve the water from the cooking, and cut it into little pieces.

Reduce the onion to puree and saute it in a frying pan with olive oil. Add the tomato, season it with salt and pepper and let it be slightly sharp. Add the octopus and chopped parsley, cook for another 5 minutes and book.

Put on a tray with olive oil. Lean slightly on each lasagna plate in the octopus cooking water and line the tray with a first layer. Have half the octopus and cover with bechamel sauce, island cheese and mozzarella cheese.

Repeat the previous process once again, covering the last layer of pasta with bechamel sauce and cheeses. Water with a thread of olive oil and bring it to the oven, preheated at 190oC, for 20 minutes.

octopus-meatballs-argiro.name: Octopus Meatballs
instructions: Place the octopus in a pot first. Cover it with water and let it simmer until it softens. Depending on the octopus, from 40 minutes up until it softens well.
Drain (we keep its juice) and after it cools the mince or grind it in the blender. Place in a bowl.
Soak the bread in the dough that boiled the octopus. Drain it well and rub it in crumbs in the octopus minced meat.
Add all the other ingredients and salt carefully, because the fresh octopus has enough salt. We ferment all the ingredients. If the dough is thin, add some toast.
Create small octopuses, flour them, shake them and brown them in hot olive oil.
We drain them into absorbent paper.

octopus-pasta.name: Octopus Pasta
instructions: Cook 12 oz. linguine in a large pot of boiling salted water, stirring occasionally, until al dente. Drain, reserving 1 cup pasta cooking liquid.

Meanwhile, heat 2 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high. Add ½ cup panko, season with kosher salt, and cook, stirring often, until golden brown and crisp, about 3 minutes. Transfer to a small bowl. Wipe out skillet.

Heat 2 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil in same skillet over medium-high. Add two 4.2-oz. cans octopus, drained, patted dry, and cook, tossing occasionally, until golden brown and crisp, 2–3 minutes. Using a slotted spoon, transfer to a plate.

Add remaining 1 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil to same skillet. Cook 5 garlic cloves, coarsely chopped, and 3 Tbsp. coarsely chopped drained capers, stirring often, until slightly softened, about 2 minutes. Add ¼ tsp. crushed red pepper flakes and a big pinch of salt; season generously with freshly ground black pepper. Cook, stirring constantly, until combined and fragrant, about 1 minute. Add 1 cup dry white wine and cook, stirring often, until reduced by two thirds, about 4 minutes.

Add pasta, ½ cup (packed) finely chopped parsley, 4 Tbsp. unsalted butter, and reserved ½ cup pasta cooking liquid to skillet; reduce heat to medium-low. Cook, tossing often and adding remaining pasta cooking liquid a little at a time as needed, until pasta is coated. Remove from heat. Add octopus, then finely grate zest from 1 lemon over. Cut lemon in half. Squeeze in juice from 1 half and toss to combine. Cut remaining half into wedges.
Divide pasta among shallow bowls and top with panko mixture. Serve with lemon wedges for squeezing over.

octopus-pilaf-argiro.name: Octopus Pilaf

ingredients.item: From a little (on the tip of a spoon) spice-carnation-candle
From a little (on the tip of a spoon) spice-carnation-candle

instructions: Clean the octopus, wash it very well and cut it into large bites.
Place the octopus in a pot over medium heat. We don't add oil.
Let it change color and drink all its liquids. After the pot dries from the octopus liquids, add the olive oil and the chopped onion.
Sauté for 3-4 minutes to become transparent without color.
Add the paste and rub it in the pot for 1 minute to bake.
Add the wine and mix. Let it evaporate.
Sprinkle with the spices and add 2 liters of hot water.
Cover and simmer for about 35 minutes until the octopus softens.
Finally, add the barley or Mirmitzelli, lower the heat and simmer for about 10 minutes until the pasta becomes pasta. Mix regularly because it works at the base of the pot.
We always make sure that the barley has enough liquid to keep it scurvy. If necessary, add some hot water.
We rub enough pepper. Salt is added after testing, because fresh octopuses are quite salty. It may not need any salt, instead add some raisin or petimese to balance the taste of the dish.
Served juicy and pimple on a deep dish.

Mom Constanza makes traditional parian pasta like a barley. Mirmitzelli is the traditional pasta of Paros and Antiparos. Make a simple dough without eggs, only with local flour some water and salt. After the dough is fermented, make fuses and dip the fingers in oil, rub between the finger and thumb the dough and give a shape of barley.

octopus-rice-traditional-recipe-teleculi.name: Octopus Rice - Traditional Recipe
instructions: Cook the octopus in boiling water for about 40 minutes. Remove from the fire and drain, reserving the broth. Cut the octopus into pieces and reserve.
Remove the skin from the tomatoes, discard the seeds and cut them into small cubes. Also clean the seed peppers and cut them into cubes. Peel and pinch the onion and garlic teeth.

Leve ao lume um tacho com o azeite, a cebola, os alhos e a folha de louro. Deixe alourar, acrescente o tomate e mexa. Adicione depois o vinho e pouco depois o caldo do polvo (cerca de 1 l). Tempere com sal e pimenta e deixe levantar fervura.

Then add the rice and peppers and let it cook in a light for about 10 minutes. If necessary, add some more water. Wrap the octopus pieces, retrieve the seasonings and remove from the fire. Add the chopped parsley and coriander, stir and serve immediately.

octopus-rice-in-the-oven-teleculinaria.name: Octopus Rice In The Oven

ingredients.item: Take to the medium heat a pot with water and let it boil.
Then add 1 coarsely chopped onion, 2 leaves of laurel and octopus. let cook for about 40 minutes, or until the octopus becomes soft (check the cooking with a fork).

instructions: Cut the octopus to pieces. Reserve.

Peel and pinch the onion; clean and cut the pepper into pieces. Praise the onion in a pot with olive oil, add the rice, wrap and refresh with white wine. Add the undone tomatoes and then the pepper and stir.
Adicione o caldo do polvo, tempere com sal e pimenta, tape e deixe cozer por cerca de 15 minutos, em lume brando. Acrescente depois o vinagre e envolva.
Transfer the rice to a tray, have on top the octopus pieces and put it in the oven, preheated at 170oC, for 8 minutes. Serve sprinkled with chopped coriander.

octopus-salad-lezzet.name: Octopus Salad
instructions: 1. Cook the pot for 45 minutes by adding water, sugar, salt and coffin.
2. Wait for the cool of the boiled ahtapot.
3. When it starts to fall & peel the shells on the floor.
4. Take the bark and service plate.
5. Dried butter small chop.
6. The yellowish is also fine shore.
7. You can relax half an hour by adding olive oil, tomato, garlic, vinegar and lemon juice.
8. Then you can serve ahtapot salad.
9.  Afiyet olsun.

octopus-salad-from-paros-argiro.name: Octopus Salad From Paros

ingredients.item: 1 cup of apple vinegar unfiltered, top
1 cup of apple vinegar unfiltered, top

instructions: We start by cleaning the octopus first, wash it and drain it.
Place it in a pot with the gum (cup) upwards.
Pour with olive oil, apple vinegar unfiltered TOP and wine.
Add the bay leaf and pepper and let them boil on medium heat for 40 minutes or until softened (depending on how well the octopus is beaten).
When it softens, we put it on a platter and so warm sprinkle it with oregano.
Once cold, cut it into bites with a knife.
Put it in a glass vessel and pour it with the juices in which it boiled.
Mix carefully to clothe each piece of it with its juices and keep it covered in the refrigerator.
When served, pour with raw olive oil and a few drops of vinegar.

octopus-salad-recipe-teleculinaria.name: Octopus Salad Recipe
instructions: Limpe os pimentos de sementes e corte-os em pequenos cubos. Descasque e pique a cebola.
Cut the boiled octopus into small pieces and put them in a bowl. Add the peppers and onion and wrap.
Tempere com o azeite, o vinagre, sal e pimenta, polvilhe com os coentros picados e volte a envolver bem. Sirva de seguida.

octopus-salad-recipe-fresh-and-light-com.name: Octopus Salad Recipe, Fresh And Light
octopus-salad-recipe-fresh-and-light-com.original_name: Receta de ensalada de pulpo, fresca y ligera

ingredients.item: 1  pulpo de entre 1 1/2 kilo a 2 kilos

instructions: To start with, you have to rinse the octopus well under the cold water group and purge it delicately.

Then the ideal thing to do is to hit the octopus with a dill or kitchen roller. That step is even more important if the octopus has been previously frozen. To start with this task, put the octopus first onto a large cutting board, or kitchen table, well clean. Be sure to pass carefully onto every space of every tentacle and of course to exclude his head.
Luego, en una olla grande, pondremos a calentar abundante agua.
When water comes to boiling point, add a handful of salt and mixture.

Now get his octopus up his head to "scare him." That's gonna get more soft. Without releasing his head, put his octopus body into his water, count to 3 and get his octopus out of his water. Repeat operation 3 or 4 times.
Luego sumerge el pulpo totalmente y agrega la hoja de laurel. Deja que el pulpo se cocine durante media hora.
Mientras el pulpo se cocina, es momento de ocuparse de los otros ingredientes. En el caso de la cebolleta, hay que limpiarla bien, escurrirla y picarla finamente, incluyendo la parte verde.
We have to do the same with parsley and put it back.
For lettuce (if you have decided to use lettuce), wash and put them back for later use.

If you decide to use the other optional vegetables, you have to leave them ready to decorate the salad as well. In the case of pimenton, it has to be clear, without seeds or vein, and we have to mingle it into thin ribbons.
En el caso de los pepinillos, hay que picarlos en rodajas delgaditas.
If you decide to use aromatic herbs, they have to be washed with advance. For example, you can use fresh basil and dill branches.
When the octopus cooking time's up, tap with a fork to see if the meat's tender enough. Otherwise, let's get ten more minutes to cook and try again if she's tender.
When the octopus's ready, put out the fire and change the place pot so that it gets cold more quickly. Let the octopus rest in the pot till at room temperature.
Then get the octopus out and scrape and put it onto a cutting plate.
Pica el pulpo en trozos de entre 2 y 3 cm. de largo.
Sazona el pulpo con pimienta al gusto, aceite de oliva al gusto y el jugo de los limones. Mezcla, y al momento de servir, espolvorea con cebollino y perejil.

If you have decided to use the other vegetables, at the time of serving the salad, make a bed with lettuce leaves and place an octopus above. Decorate with pimenton and pickle and select some leaves of aromatic herbs at the centre.
Si no vas a servir la ensalada de pulpo de inmediato, puedes conservar el pulpo en el refrigerador y sacarlo algunos minutos antes.

octopus-salad-with-black-eyes-argiro.name: Octopus Salad With Black Eyes

ingredients.item: 1 dry onion small cut into rings
Little bit of chopped red pepper
1 dry onion small cut into rings
Little bit of chopped red pepper

instructions: We start by simmering the octopus in a small pot with oil, pepper, wine and vinegar, until it softens well.
Let it cool and cut it into bites.
On a platter, place the lettuce coarsely chopped
Add the beans, onion rings, radishes cut into slices, cucumbers and octopus bites.
Sprinkle with parsley and pour the salad with the zum in which the octopus boiled.

octopus-salad-with-chickpeas-and-pepper-.name: Octopus Salad With Chickpeas And Pepper
octopus-salad-with-chickpeas-and-pepper-.original_name: Salada de polvo com grão e pimento
instructions: Place the octopus in a pot, add the peeled onion and the blonde leaf and cover with water. Take it to the fire, let it boil and boil for 40 minutes, or until the octopus becomes soft and tender.

Brush the octopus, let it cool and cut it into pieces; cut the tomato into small pieces; peel the cucumber and cut it into cubes; clean the peppers and cut them into cubes as well. Put the vegetables in a salad bowl.

Add the leek, cut into thick slices, and the grain, previously washed and drained. Add the octopus, season with salt and pepper, water with olive oil and sprinkle with chopped parsley. Mix gently and serve.

octopus-stew-lezzet.name: Octopus Stew

ingredients.item: 1 piece of dry onion (cut in large par&lectors)
1 red onion ( cut into large par&lectors)

instructions: 1. After cooking at least 1 hour high steam with vinegar and 800 ml of water in the water, cut the legs of the octopus on the finger neck.
2. You can also use it if you want the head, but the head will not be soft as the legs.
3. In a deep pot (mellow) onion and yellowish medium - in the olive oil for 10 minutes.
4. The color of the onions is lightly transparent, add the butter and pepper in the middle-high fire for 5 minutes to continue to be accompanied.
5. U add ahtapot, green lemon juice and bark, ça variety of black peppers, salt, bay leaf, flower seed, and continue to hang all the materials together for 5 minutes.
6.   Then add the boiled water of wine and ahtapot, potatoes and green olives.
7. Bake 40-45 minutes in jealous fire by closing the cover of the pot and serve hot.

octopus-stew-in-the-oven-argiro.name: Octopus Stew In The Oven

ingredients.item: 5 medium onions cut into thick slices
6 medium potatoes cut into thick pieces
5 medium onions cut into thick slices
6 medium potatoes cut into thick pieces

instructions: Clean the octopus first and then marinate. If we use frozen octopus, let it thaw properly into the refrigerator maintenance.
Cut the octopus into tentacles.
Add the olive oil and vinegar.
Strain the orange.
Add the peel from the orange, spices and herbs.
Mix, cover and set aside to marinate.
In a deep pan, add the olive oil, heat it over medium heat.
Cut the onions into thick slices and add them to the hot oil.
Sauté for 7-8 minutes, stirring until the onion becomes transparent and slowly starts caramelizing.
Break the garlic and add them to the pan, sauté for 1 minute, stirring.
Add the paste and rub it on the base of the pan.
Bake for 1 minute and add the vinegar and allow the ingredients to dry.
Finally, add the wine and turn off the stove.
Clean the potatoes and cut them into thick slices.
In a deep baking pan add the potatoes in a layer, add the saved onions and all the liquids in the pan.
Add the tomatoes cut into thick pieces.
Finally, enter the marinated octopus tentacles into the materials.
Add any liquid left over from the marinade.
Add salt and pepper and mix the ingredients. Attention! Salt by measure because octopus can already be salty.
Cover the baking pan with non-stick paper and seal around the baking pan with aluminum foil.
Alternatively you can use a pot with lid.

Bake in a well preheated oven for 1 hour at 170 degrees in air. Uncover the baking pan and continue baking for about 35 more minutes, until the octopus stew stays almost with its oil, softens and melts the potatoes by sucking all the octopus zooms.
Serve the octopus stew in the oven with melted potatoes and season salad.

octopus-tentacle-on-parchment-paper-argi.name: Octopus Tentacle On Parchment Paper
instructions: Wash and drain the octopus well.
We separate the octopus tentacles. They must be fat to make the octopus grow juicy on the paper.
In a multi we prepare the marinade. Add the olive oil, water, lemon juice, paprika and pepper to the blender and beat.
Place the tentacles in a bowl, add the tomato in slices and fennel.
Mix the ingredients and allow the octopus to marinate for 10 minutes.
However, prepare the parchment paper cases to seal the octopus inside with all the materials.
We will use simple flour and flour and water to seal the packages well.
Mix some flour in a bowl and add some water until we have a porridge like glue.
In a baking pan we put a large piece of anti-stick paper.
Seal with flour glue and create a case with opening above.
Allow it to dry for a while and stick the parchment paper compounds together.
The same way we make a second package.
After the openings are stabilised in the envelopes from non-stick paper, we equalize the octopus to 2 packs.
We balance the marinade zooms in the two packages.
Add a few slices of flint, fresh onion and a leaf of leek.

Seal the envelope with flour glue and fold well to stabilise the opening in the package and keep the liquids from running out. Make sure that by making the parchment packs there is plenty of room left around because by baking they inflate like a balloon.
Place it in a preheated oven and bake for 35 minutes.
After the octopus is cooked, remove it from the oven and leave it in the package for 10 minutes.
Place the tentacles on a platter and pour some of their zucchini.
Add some olive oil and serve them warm or cold.
The octopus in the parchment paper can be cut into pieces and preserved in a jar in oil for a few weeks. So you'll have a delicious snack with octopus every moment.

octopus-vinegar-argiro.name: Octopus Vinegar
instructions: We start by cleaning the octopus.
Wash it very well under running water and let it drain.
In a pot, place the octopus with the hood upwards.
Pour it with olive oil, vinegar and wine.
Add the bay leaf and pepper and let them boil over medium heat.
From the time it gets boiled, about 40 minutes or until it softens (depending on how well it is beaten).
When it gets soft, we put it on a platter.

octopus-with-artichokes-and-dill-argiro.name: Octopus With Artichokes And Dill
instructions: We start by putting the octopus first in a pot with wine and water just covering it.
Cover and simmer until it softens well.
Clean the artichokes, removing all their hard leaves.
Cut them into slices and add them to water with lemon.
Add the fresh and dry onions to a pan for 1-2 minutes.
Add the artichokes to sauté.
Cut the octopus into pieces and add it to the pan along with all the remaining liquids in the pot.
Rub a lot of pepper, cover and let the food simmer for 15 minutes on low heat until its liquids are saved.
To the end of the boil sprinkle with the chopped dill.
Learn how to choose and how to cook the octopus properly for delicious foods.

octopus-with-fava-beans-argiro.name: Octopus With Fava Beans
instructions: We need a small clay vessel that fits the octopus.
This is essential, so they don't spread the fluids we use.
Add the octopus to the clay and pour it with vinegar.
Cover it with membrane and leave for an hour.
Drain and throw the vinegar.
Add the bay, pepper, garlic and oregano to the clay and cover the octopus with olive oil.
Cover with parchment paper and foil very well, sealing around.
Bake in a well preheated oven for 45 minutes.
After the first 20 minutes, reveal and turn the octopus. 'Cause the cooking's picking up.
Seal the clay again and continue baking for 25 minutes extra.
After baking, remove it from its juice, cut it into pieces and serve it with a married fava.
Pour with the baking sauces.

octopus-with-honey-argiro.name: Octopus With Honey
instructions: Clean the octopus: remove the mouth from the center, flip the hood, empty the interiors, wash well and drain. Cut it into pieces of large bite size.
Clean the onions and cross them.
In a wide shallow pot, heat the olive oil over medium heat and add the onions and garlic.
Sauté for about 10 minutes, stirring regularly, until slightly golden, soften and become transparent.
Add the octopus, turn the heat on and sauté for 5 minutes, stirring frequently.
Once changed, add the balsamic vinegar, wine and cognac.
Add the tomato crucified and add it to the pot along with the laurel, the spice and a lot of freshly ground pepper.
Once the boil starts, lower the fire. Cover and simmer for about 11⁄2 hours.
To the end of cooking, add the honey and mix gently.
Serve the octopus warm, accompanied by crispy crispy potatoes or French fries.

octopus-with-juicy-broth-argiro.name: Octopus With Juicy Broth
instructions: Place the octopus in a small pot just enough to fit it.
Add the wine, vinegar, pepper, onion and let it boil.
Cover and boil in moderate intensity until it softens around 40’.
After boiling, strain its broth and fill in water so that we have 1 litre of liquids.
Cut the octopus into thick slices

octopus-with-potatoes-argiro.name: Octopus With Potatoes
instructions: Rinse the octopus first and cut it into pieces larger than bites.
Place it in a pot with vinegar and simmer for 15 minutes until it drinks all its liquids.
Then add the olive oil and onions cut into thick slices and garlic cloves for the yahni octopus.
Leave them on low heat for 10 minutes until they melt (not make color).
Clean the potatoes and cut them with quinces.
Add them to the octopus and mix slightly just enough to oil.
Pour with the tomatoes and add the honey, the bay leaves, the spice and lots of pepper.
Add a little water, just half the food.
Simmer for about 35 minutes until the potatoes and octopus soften and the sauce melts.
Try the yachni octopus and if it needs salt add.

octopus-with-spicy-spaghetti-argiro.name: Octopus With Spicy Spaghetti
instructions: Clean the octopus (turn its hood and remove its interior, also press and remove its mouth from the center).
Wash the octopus well and cut it with a knife in large bites.
In a medium pot, add the oil and onions.
Sauté for 5 minutes, stirring until the onions are polished and transparent.
Place the octopus pieces in the pot and let them cook over medium heat.
At this stage it will take a nice red dark color and enhance its taste.
Sprinkle with the spices and mix to remove their perfume.
Add the tomato paste, the laurel and rub in the pot, cook and smell.
Add the ingredients with the wine and degrave (grab) the base of the pot.
After the alcohol evaporates for a few minutes, add the ground tomatoes, as well as enough water, so that it covers our ingredients.
Allow the food covered to simmer until the octopus softens well. About 35 to 40 minutes.
Add the cut pasta and if necessary, some extra water (to boil the pasta).
Simmer on low heat for 10 minutes, stirring regularly until the cut macaroni boils and the food stays with a little zoom.

Check if it needs salt and rub pepper. Especially if your octopus is fresh, you may not have to salt. It's pretty salty sometimes. Instead, you may need to add a pinch of sugar to balance the flavors).
Remove the pot from the heat while the food is still juicy and add two ice cubes to drop the temperature and stop boiling the pasta.
Serve while it is still warm and juicy. If we want, sprinkle with some parsley.

octopus-with-teriyaki-sauce-on-lentil-fa.name: Octopus With Teriyaki Sauce (on Lentil Fava Bed)
instructions: 1. Goodly washed all the cookware with shredded rounds, on top of boiling water in steamed cooking pot, 2-2.
2. Cook in steam up to 5 hours.
3. Wait for the oda room temperature after softening well.
4. On the other hand, start preparing lentilmek fava.
5. Goodly washed into the yellow lentilm 1 onions, 2 teeth garlic, lemon juice, sugar and salt, add water until a finger on the top and slowly boil.
6. Goodly pull well with a robot when it becomes softening and lentilizing, unload and leave it to be stacked.
7. Cut the floor into thick slices, roast up to 5 minutes with olive oil and teriyaki sauce in a fried teflon pan.
8. Then serve with mountain cake on the bed from lentilmek fava.

octopus-with-tomato-sauce-lezzet.name: Octopus With Tomato Sauce
instructions: 1. First of all, take a cleaned wood pot, add some water on it.
2. Cook for 20 minutes by adding vinegar, kimyon and bay leaf in addition to Ahtapota.
3. Wash potatoes and keres and chop potatoes after peeling.
4. slice the peninsula in a fine way.
5. Roast potatoes and kere in different pots.
6. For anatesis sauce; mix it by adding anatesis crush into hot candlestick sauce.
7. Mix on adding cream and oil.
8. Then add pepper and salt.
9. Mix some powdery pepper by adding.
10. After mixing materials, pass through the strainer.
11. 11. For tomato sauce, repel tomatoes and get to the pan.
12. You can also add fine chopped onions.
12. August After cooking tomato sauce in the pan, crush it from the strainer and get a light juicy consistency.
14 July Once the pizzan is completely leaked, then in small parts, the plate and the service plate will be sided.
15. Index of uncleed potatoes in addition to Octopuss.
16. Add tomatoes and anoresis sauce on it.
17. September Serve Octopus and potatoes in the same plate.
18. Never mix before.
19.  Afiyet olsun.
20. What are the Tricks of Octopus Cooking? You can swipe directly if the ahtapot is cleaned and frozen freshly.
21. The ice cream process allows the ahtapot to soften enough.
22. Small ahtapots are cooked in the average 30 minutes, while the ahtapots weighing 20, 1 kilo.
23. If cooked more than the requirement of Octopus, the flesh is hardened.

oddfellows-waffle-cones.name: Oddfellows' Waffle Cones
instructions: Whisk flour, salt, cinnamon, and ¾ cup powdered sugar in a medium bowl. Using an electric mixer on medium-high speed, beat egg whites and remaining 2 Tbsp. powdered sugar in a large bowl to stiff, fluffy peaks. Fold dry ingredients into egg white mixture until just blended. Fold in butter and mix until all is incorporated.

Spray a waffle-cone maker with nonstick spray and heat to medium. Using ¼ cup batter per batch, make waffle cones one at a time and according to manufacturer’s instructions, baking to desired doneness and molding them while still warm.

ode-to-halo-halo.name: Ode To Halo-halo
instructions: Combine bananas, brown sugar, vanilla, a pinch of salt, and 2 Tbsp. water in a small bowl. Let sit, stirring occasionally, until bananas are jammy and liquid is slightly thickened, 20–30 minutes.

Meanwhile, combine berries, lime juice, granulated sugar, and a pinch of salt in a medium bowl. Mash berries lightly to release juices. Let sit, stirring occasionally, until juices are slightly thickened, 15–20 minutes.

Stir coconut milk and sweetened condensed milk in a small bowl to combine. Assemble halo-halo in 2 small bowls or pint glasses, alternating layers of bananas, berries, shaved ice, ice cream, and coconut milk mixture. Top as desired.

oeufs-cocotte-baked-eggs.name: Oeufs Cocotte (baked Eggs)

ingredients.item: Salt and ground black pepper to taste

instructions: Preheat the oven to 191°C (190 degrees C). Grease a small, lidded casserole dish with olive oil. Boil water in a kettle and set aside.

Arrange ham in the bottom of the prepared casserole dish. Sprinkle with 1 tablespoon Gruyère cheese. Pour in cream and season with black pepper. Crack eggs on top, one at a time, making sure yolks remain intact. Sprinkle with remaining 1 tablespoon Gruyère cheese and chives. Season with salt and black pepper.
Cover the casserole dish and place it into a roasting pan. Pour enough boiled water into the roasting pan so it reaches about halfway up the sides of the casserole dish.
Bake in the preheated oven for 9 minutes. Uncover and continue baking until whites are just set and yolks are still runny, 3 to 6 more minutes.

of-both-worlds-lobster-roll.name: Of-both-worlds Lobster Roll

ingredients.item: New england–style split-top hot dog buns

instructions: Fit a large pot with a steamer basket and pour in water to come to top of basket. Place over high heat. If desired, dispatch lobsters by using a sharp chef’s knife (and one fell swoop) to split the front section of each between the eyes. Place lobsters in pot, cover, and steam (the pot should be actively filled with steam) until shells are bright red and tails are curled, 6–8 minutes. Let sit until cool enough to handle, 12–15 minutes.
Meanwhile, mix mayonnaise, celery, lemon zest, lemon juice, and 1 Tbsp. chives in a medium bowl to combine; season dressing with salt and pepper.

Remove lobster meat from shells and cut into large pieces; discard shells. Melt butter in a large saucepan over medium heat; transfer 2 Tbsp. melted butter to a small bowl and set aside for brushing buns. Add lobster meat to remaining butter in saucepan and toss to coat. Cook, tossing occasionally, until just warmed through (it should not be hot; you don’t want it to overcook), about 4 minutes. Season with salt, then spoon lobster meat into bowl with dressing with a slotted spoon and gently toss to coat.
Heat a dry large skillet over medium. Brush reserved butter over outsides of buns. Toast, buttered side down, until golden, about 3 minutes per side.

Line each bun with a piece of lettuce and spoon in dressed lobster; top with more chives.
Do Ahead: Lobsters can be cooked and shelled and dressing can be made 1 day ahead. Cover and chill separately.

off-the-radar.name: Off The Radar
instructions: Add gin, lemon juice, maraschino liqueur, and liqueur de violette to a Collins glass filled with ice. Add tonic water; stir to combine. Garnish with a lemon wheel.

off-the-wagon.name: Off The Wagon

ingredients.prep: cut into wedges, plus 4 wedges for garnish

instructions: Prepare grill for medium-high heat. Grill plums until caramelized and lightly charred, about 1 minute per side; let cool.

Muddle 2 plums in a large pitcher until coarsely mashed. Add bourbon, vermouth, lemon juice, honey, and bitters and fill pitcher with ice. Stir until outside of pitcher is frosty, about 1 minute. Strain into 4 Old Fashioned glasses filled with ice; discard solids. Garnish each with a plum wedge.

og-zuppa-toscana-soup.name: og' Zuppa Toscana Soup
instructions: Combine sausage and red pepper flakes in a skillet over medium heat; cook and stir until sausage is brown and crumbly, 7 to 10 minutes. Drain excess fat; transfer sausage to a bowl and set aside.
Cook onion, bacon, and garlic in the same skillet over medium-low heat until bacon is crisp and onions are soft, 10 to 15 minutes. Set aside.
Combine water and chicken bouillon in a large pot; bring to a boil. Stir in potatoes; cook until soft, about 15 minutes.
Pour in cream; simmer until heated through, about 2 minutes. Add sausage, onion mixture, and kale; cook and stir until flavors combine, about 10 minutes.

oh-arctic-char.name: Oh Arctic Char!
instructions: Preheat the oven to 204°C (200 degrees C). Line a baking dish with aluminum foil.
Season arctic char with sea salt, place in the prepared baking dish, and sprinkle with lime zest.
Combine lime juice, olive oil, sherry, rosemary, and garlic in a food processor; pulse until garlic is finely chopped.
Spread mixture over fish; season with cayenne pepper and black pepper.

Bake in the preheated oven until fish flakes easily with a fork, 12 to 15 minutes, basting with pan juices about halfway through. When fish is close to being fully cooked, switch the oven to broil. Cook until the top is browned, about 2 minutes.

oh-fuddgge-fudge.name: oh Fuddgge' Fudge
instructions: Grease a 9x13-inch pan.

Combine sugar, margarine, evaporated milk, and salt in a medium saucepan. Bring to a full rolling boil. Boil for 5 minutes, stirring constantly, until fudge reaches 112°C (112 degrees C) on a candy thermometer.
Remove from heat. Add marshmallow cream, chocolate chips, nuts, and vanilla extract; mix well until blended. Pour into the prepared pan and refrigerate until firm.
Cut into seventy-eight 1 1/2-inch squares.

oil-and-lemon-sauce-argiro.name: Oil And Lemon Sauce
instructions: In a bowl, mix the olive oil with lemon juice.
For liquid oil lemon, tap for a while with a fork or wire.
For creamy lime, put the materials in a jar with a lid and hit hard or use rhableder.
If the olive oil is intended for very fatty fish, you can increase lemon for a taste balance, depending on personal taste.

oil-and-vinegar-potato-salad.name: Oil And Vinegar Potato Salad
instructions: Cook potatoes in large pot of boiling salted water until tender, about 30 minutes. Drain. Cool to just warm, 10 to 15 minutes; peel potatoes. Cut each potato in half, then cut into 1/3-inch slices.

Place warm potatoes in large bowl. Stir red wine vinegar and sugar in small bowl until sugar dissolves, then drizzle over potatoes. Toss to coat. Mix in onion slices. Season to taste with salt and pepper. Cool to room temperature.
Add olive oil and parsley to potatoes. Toss well and let marinate at least 20 minutes and up to 2 hours at room temperature.

oil-bread-recipe-comedera.name: Oil Bread, Recipe
instructions: In a bowl, you'll place the flour, salt and yeast. Fold and then integrate olive oil and stir with a spatula.
Later, you will add water and mix up until you have a homogenous and manageable mass.
You'll put a ball of your dough and put it into a clear bowl and cover it with a little cloth and leave it up for 30 minutes.

Filling up your cover and you'll put up the dough to stretch very well. Kill her for 5 minutes to remove the gases. You'll make her a ball again, put her in a bowl, cover her with a little cloth and come our second lifting process. That's an hour.
After this time, you will take your dough and shape the oil bread. You can make a full or a few portions. I recommend you make a big one so you can cut the slices.

Once you have put your bread into shape, you will place it on a plate with a kitchen paper, and brush with oil the whole surface. In this process you will let them rise for 30 more minutes or till they double their size.
A few minutes to complete this lifting process, preheat the oven at 220 degrees Celsius.
The bread will be baked for about 25 minutes till it's golden and perfectly ready.
We're done!

oil-cookies-for-fasting-argiro.name: Oil Cookies For Fasting
instructions: Mix all the liquid ingredients, oil, raki, cognac in a large bowl.
Add the sugar and mix with the hand or wire to melt the sugar.
Dissolve the soda in the orange juice.
Add the orange to the liquid ingredients.
Mix with a spoon all the solid ingredients, flour, baking, cinnamon puffs in a bowl and add them to the liquids.
Gently ferment the mixture to gather in a fluffy, soft and malleable dough.
We make little cookies in whatever shape we want.
Dive them into the sesame and add them to sheeting.

oil-nuts-argiro.name: Oil Nuts
instructions: In a bowl, add the oil and sugar.
By hand, stir and slowly with the heat of the hand the sugar begins to melt.
Dissolve the soda in the juice, mix to activate and foam.
Put them in the oil.
Add the ouzo, salt, zest, anise and mix gently.
Finally, add the flour and knead until the dough is removed from the walls and a dough ball is molded, with oily and soft texture.
Leave the dough covered in the bowl for 20′ to rest.
We put her on the counter and split her in two places.
Ferment lightly.
We make two strawberries long like two thick dough fuses.
Sprinkle with some water and sprinkle the sesame.
We roll the strawberries into the sesame so they can dress around.
Put the strawberries on a baking pan lined with non-stick paper.
Bake in the air on the medium grill at 170 °C for 30 ́, to brown on the outside and smell.
In the center the dough at this stage will remind you of baked cake.
They're not yet crispy but they're baked and their perfume is intoxicating.
We're letting five minutes-10's of the strawberries get lukewarm so we can catch them.
With a knife, cut the soft sweet bread into slices with a thickness of 1.5 cm.
So we're just going to take them all out and we're going to dry them all to make them crispy and fluffy.
We touch the cut nuts on the baking pan, close to each other.
The cut side must be on the baking pan.
Bake at 160 °C in the air, in the middle grill, for 30′ to brown and dry completely.

oil-of-parsley-dill-mint-argiro.name: Oil Of Parsley, Dill, Mint
instructions: First wash the herbs and dry them well.
Tie them in a small bottle and put them in a bottle with a wide mouth.
Add the whole garlic cloves and peppers.
Cover with olive oil.
Cover the bottle well and store the oil in a cool, dark place or in the refrigerator for 2 weeks.
Then we strain it because these herbs are sensitive and will spoil.

oil-poached-swordfish-with-white-corn-gu.name: Oil-poached Swordfish With White Corn, Guanciale And Chive Oil
instructions: Combine oil and chives in blender and puree until smooth. Season chive oil to taste with salt and pepper.

Cook corn in large pot of boiling salted water until just tender, about 8 minutes. Using tongs, remove corn from cooking liquid. Place on plate; reserve 1/2 cup corn cooking liquid. Cut corn kernels from cobs and reserve 4 cups (save remaining corn for another use).

Preheat oven to 177°C. Combine oil, lemon, crushed garlic, and rosemary in medium ovenproof pot. Attach deep-fry thermometer to side of pot; heat oil to 66°C. Carefully lower swordfish into oil. Transfer pot to oven; poach fish uncovered until just firm to touch, about 15 minutes.

Meanwhile, heat large nonstick skillet over medium heat. Add guanciale; sauté until almost crisp, 2 to 3 minutes. Add minced garlic and shallot; sauté until soft but not brown, about 3 minutes. Stir in reserved 4 cups corn and crushed red pepper. Add butter and 2 tablespoons reserved corn cooking liquid; stir until mixture is moist and creamy, adding more cooking liquid by tablespoonfuls if dry, about 5 minutes. Stir in chives and thyme. Season with sea salt and pepper.
Divide corn among 4 shallow bowls. Top each with swordfish steak. Sprinkle with sea salt; drizzle with chive oil.

oil-poached-tomatoes.name: Oil-poached Tomatoes
instructions: Preheat oven to 300°. Toss tomatoes, garlic, rosemary and thyme sprigs, oil, and salt in a large baking dish.
Bake tomatoes until they are soft and skins begin to shrivel, 35–45 minutes. Let cool slightly, then slip off skins. Discard herbs.
DO AHEAD: Tomatoes can be made 5 days ahead. Cover tomatoes and oil and ­chill.

oil-poached-tuna-salad.name: Oil-poached Tuna Salad

ingredients.prep: soaked overnight, drained, or one 15-ounce can navy beans

ingredients.item: . chopped fresh dill plus small sprigs for serving
Small sun gold or cherry tomatoes

instructions: A deep-fry thermometer

Bring flageolet beans and 4 cups water to a boil in a large saucepan, reduce heat, and simmer until beans are tender, 50–60 minutes; season with salt. Let beans cool; drain. Transfer to a medium bowl. (Do not cook canned beans.)

Cook green beans in another large saucepan of boiling salted water until crisp-tender, about 5 minutes. Drain and transfer to a colander set in a bowl of ice water. Let cool; drain and set aside. Wipe saucepan dry.

Heat oil, rosemary, and garlic in same saucepan over medium-low heat until deep-fry thermometer registers 160°. Season tuna with salt and pepper and cook in oil until opaque except for a bit of pink in the center, about 4 minutes per side. Using a slotted spatula, transfer tuna to a plate. Let poaching oil cool, then remove rosemary and garlic. Set aside ¼ cup oil.

Whisk anchovies, shallot, parsley, lemon juice, mustard, chopped dill, and reserved poaching oil in a small bowl; season with salt, pepper, and more lemon juice, if desired. Add some vinaigrette to bowl with flageolet beans and toss to coat.
Break tuna into large pieces and arrange on a platter with flageolet and green beans, tomatoes, and olives. Top with a few dill sprigs and serve with remaining vinaigrette alongside.
DO AHEAD: Tuna can be made 1 day ahead. Return fish to cooled oil, cover, and chill. Serve at room temperature.

oily-pepper-pickle-recipe-lezzet.name: Oily Pepper Pickle Recipe
instructions: 1. Wash the peppers, wash the stems and the r remove the rough
2. Peel the garlic, all or fine slice.
3. Mix liquid oil in a container of vinegar, water and salt.
4. Kavanozu temizleyip kuru hâle getirin.
5. Place the peppers in the jar, add garlic to their interval.
6. The liquid mixture is completely covered by peppers.
7. Keep the jars from 2–3 days at room temperature.
8. Then leave at least 10 g&uuml in a cool and dark place and serve.
9.  Afiyet olsun.

okasans-japanese-steamed-salmon.name: Okasan's Japanese Steamed Salmon
instructions: Quarter or halve salmon side; set aside.
Combine chicken broth, ginger, soy sauce, minced onion, mirin, and sesame oil in a large, heavy pot over medium-high heat; bring to a boil, then reduce heat to a simmer.

Place a steamer insert into the pot; arrange salmon in a single layer in the insert. Cover pot; steam, 5 to 7 minutes, checking for desired doneness. Transfer salmon to a platter; keep warm. Remove insert.
Boil broth mixture until reduced into a sauce consistency, 2 to 4 minutes; spoon over salmon.

oklahoma-indian-tacos.name: Oklahoma Indian Tacos

ingredients.item: Diced tomatoes with green chile peppers

instructions: Preheat the oven to 93°C (95 degrees C).
Cook and stir ground beef and onion in a large skillet over medium heat until beef is brown and crumbly, about 10 minutes; drain excess grease.

Stir in beans, diced tomatoes, diced tomatoes with green chiles, and chili seasoning; bring to a boil. Reduce heat to low; simmer until thick, about 10 minutes. Continue simmering while making fry bread.
Combine self-rising flour and buttermilk in a bowl (dough will be very sticky). Scoop about ¼ cup dough; gently pat into a 5-inch circle on a well-floured work surface. Repeat with remaining dough.
Heat vegetable oil in a heavy skillet to 204°C (205 degrees C).

Fry 1 dough circle until golden brown, about 3 minutes per side. Fry bread will expand in size as it cooks. Drain on paper towels; place on a baking sheet. Transfer to the oven to keep warm while frying remaining dough pieces.
Top fried bread with ground beef mixture; serve.

okonomiyaki.name: Okonomiyaki

ingredients.item: . instant dashi powder or fish sauce

ingredients.prep: very thinly sliced (use a mandoline if you have one; about 3 cups)

ingredients.item: Mayonnaise (such as kewpie) and furikake or sesame seeds

instructions: Whisk 2 large eggs, one 1" piece ginger, peeled, finely grated, 2 garlic cloves, finely grated, ⅓ cup all-purpose flour, 1 Tbsp. instant dashi powder or fish sauce, 1 Tbsp. soy sauce, a pinch of kosher salt, and ½ cup water in a large bowl until smooth. Add ⅓ large head of cabbage (about 12 oz.), very thinly sliced (about 3 cups), and 1 small onion, very finely chopped; mix vigorously to coat.

Line bottom of a dry 10"-diameter nonstick skillet with 5 bacon slices, halved crosswise, in as even a layer as possible. Set over medium-high heat, then pile cabbage mixture on top of bacon and flatten into an even layer. Cook, tucking in sides and patting mixture with a rubber spatula to keep it in an even round and shaking pan occasionally, until bacon is browned and crisp,7–9 minutes.

Place a large plate upside down over pan and carefully flip plate and pan over in one quick motion so okonomiyaki is turned out onto plate, bacon side up (do this over the sink to catch any drips). Slide back into pan and cook until set on the outside but still tender inside, 6–8 minutes. Carefully transfer to a clean plate.

Mix ½ cup ketchup, 1 Tbsp. Worcestershire sauce, and remaining 1 Tbsp. soy sauce in a small bowl to combine. Squeeze mayonnaise over okonomiyaki as desired, then drizzle seasoned ketchup over. Sprinkle some furikake or sesame seeds on top.

okonomiyaki-brussels-sprouts.name: Okonomiyaki Brussels Sprouts
instructions: Arrange oven racks in top and lower thirds of oven. Place one rimmed baking sheet on top rack and another on bottom rack; preheat to 450°. Toss brussels sprouts and oil in a large bowl; season with salt. Carefully remove one hot pan from oven and transfer half of sprouts to pan—they’ll sizzle! Working quickly, turn sprouts cut sides down (don't worry if you can't get to all of them; it doesn't have to be perfect!). Repeat with second pan and remaining sprouts. Roast brussels sprouts, rotating pans halfway through, until cut sides are seared and tops are browned, 15–20 minutes.

Meanwhile, mix Worcestershire sauce, ketchup, honey, vinegar, ginger, and ½ tsp. shichimi togarashi in a small pot. Bring to a simmer over medium-high heat, then reduce heat to medium-low and gently simmer, stirring frequently, until thickened and glazy, 3–4 minutes. Taste and season with salt.

Pour glaze over brussels sprouts and toss to coat. Transfer to a platter. Sprinkle with nori, then squeeze mayonnaise over in stripes, covering the whole dish (trust us!). Pile bonito flakes in the center of the sprouts. Top with sesame seeds, scallions, and a dusting of togarashi.

okra-chips-recipe-lezzet.name: Okra Chips Recipe

ingredients.item: half a month of garlic or 1 toothpick (optional)
You can use delicious bam instead of fresh bam, but it is necessary to dry well solved.
You can try a different taste for green beans or pumpkin slices as chips.
Coconut oil instead of olive oil, avocado oil or sunflower oil can be used; each add different flavor.
A lighter version can reduce oil slightly.
Apart from salt and pepper, you can get different tastes by adding kimyon, thyme, red powder pepper or garlic powder.
If you thoroughly eat the spices from the oven &ouml to the bamya, every side of the chips becomes tasty.
After spraying, you can add a fresh aroma by walking light lemon juice or vinegar to the chips &uuml.
You can prepare a salty and aromatic chips by adding a little grated parmesan cheese.

instructions: 1. Wash and dry the legs and cut the stem parts.
2. Fine long or cut into small parts.
3. Mix olive oil, salt, pepper, pepper and garlic powder in a bowl.
4. Add to the spicy oil mixture and blend well.
5. Cover the oven tray with oily paper and single row hâlinde index.
6. From 180° to 20–25 minutes in the oven, bake with intermediate mixing; bamians shouldn.
7. Wait to cool a little after taking the oven and serve.

okra-dish-with-olive-oil-lezzet.name: Okra Dish With Olive Oil
instructions: 1. Clean the stems of Bamians with a sharp bçakla, cut to the cone leg.
2. Then wash in plenty of water and strain.
3. Finely shore onions and garlic.
4. Relate the shells of tomatoes after peeling.
5. In the i&ccedil of the pot, fry the olive oil.
6. By adding onions and yellowms to the car, roast to pink.
7. Then look at the salç until the breadth and reindeed stones.
8. When you start to keep your meal, the mixture is poured in it.
9. After adding hot water and salt, close the cover of the pot.
10. Bake about 20 minutes in medium fire.
11. 11. When the mammals begin to soften, add lemon juice and powder sugar.
12. After cooking for 5 minutes, take it from fire and leave it cool.
13.  Afiyet olsun.

okra-gumbo.name: Okra Gumbo

ingredients.item: . bone-in beef short ribs or shanks

instructions: Preheat oven to 375°. Combine paprika, salt, black pepper, and cayenne pepper in a small bowl. Season short ribs with 1½ tsp. spice mixture. Place in a shallow baking pan and roast until browned and fat has rendered, about 45 minutes.

Transfer bones to a large Dutch oven or heavy pot. Discard fat. Add stew meat, bay leaves, and 2 qt. water. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer 2 hours (the broth develops stronger flavor the longer you let it simmer). Using tongs, remove bones. When cool enough to handle, trim any meat from bones and return meat to pot. Discard fat and bones.

Heat bacon fat in a large skillet over medium-high. Add okra and cook, stirring occasionally, until lightly browned, 7–10 minutes. Add onion, bell pepper, and celery and cook, stirring frequently, until well softened, 10 minutes. Add tomatoes, garlic, chile pepper, thyme, and remaining spice mixture and cook 5 minutes more.

Add okra mixture to Dutch oven and simmer, adding crab and/or shrimp during the last 5 minutes of cooking time (if using), until okra is tender and flavors have melded, 20–30 minutes. Discard bay leaves. Taste to adjust seasonings. Serve with rice alongside.

okra-in-the-oven-with-tomato-sauce-argir.name: Okra In The Oven With Tomato Sauce
okra-in-the-oven-with-tomato-sauce-argir.original_name: Μπάμιες στο φούρνο με σάλτσα ντομάτας
instructions: Wash the okra and let them drain completely from the water for a while.
Then clean them and salt them. Spread them into a large baking pan, sprinkle them with vinegar and mix.
Leave them in the sun or in a hot oven for one hour. To eliminate the sticky substance.
After this process, we drain them well in a colander to remove all the liquids.
We never wash them from vinegar and salt, because they will extract more sticky substance externally.
Cut the tomatoes into thick pieces, add them to a small pot and boil (without olive oil) over medium to high heat for 15 minutes, until they drink all their liquids and become almost like customers.
When the sauce is completely dry in the pot and has no liquids, add the olive oil.
Once warm, add the garlic cut into thin slices of sugar and some salt and pepper.
Lower the heat and simmer for 10 minutes. We watch the salt in the sauce because the okra is already salted.
Heat a large pan or pot on high heat, without adding olive oil, add the okra to the hot dry vessel and leave them for about 10 minutes, until completely dry, stirring regularly to help the process.
When they drink all their liquids, add 1⁄4 cup. olive oil and sauté the okra over medium to strong heat for 2-3 minutes.
Add the onion and sauté for 3-4 minutes to become transparent.
Add the paste and rub it with a wooden spoon at the base of the vessel for 1 minute, to bake and remove its flavors.

okra-oilers-argiro.name: Okra Oilers
instructions: Clean the okra with a small sharp knife and remove the casing around the head as if we peel them conically. We make sure that their little head stays on top of the okra and doesn't open or rip.
After we're done, we wash them and drain them well.
Put them in a baking pan, pour them with vinegar and sprinkle them with the thick salt. Mix them lightly so the vinegar goes everywhere.
Let them stand for about an hour. If it's summer, we put them in the strong sun.

Why do I marinate them in vinegar and salt? This marinating especially under the hot summer sun will help them stay whole and not run the sticky liquid from inside and their seeds come out in cooking. The result? In cooking they will be sweeter with percussion flesh.

instructions: 1. Carefully cut the stem parts of the legs in the form of cone.
